methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $350M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
GrayMatter Robotics, founded by Ariyan Kabir & Brual Shah, is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $70M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $300M. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable across the robotics sector.

Two triangulation methods are applied here. Stage-progression analysis, using the typical 1.9x multiplier for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $570M from the $300M base. Peer-median analysis across five same-stage, same-sector peers—1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yields a median of $1.6B. The peer range spans $350M to $10.0B, reflecting material dispersion in the cohort.

Synthesizing these methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $484M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median estimate of $1.1B. The gap between stage-progression ($570M) and peer-median ($1.6B) reflects substantial uncertainty around GrayMatter's positioning within the peer set. Key variables driving valuation include relative market traction, unit economics, and technology differentiation versus the high-performing outliers like 1X. The wide peer range suggests comparable-company methodology carries heightened model risk in this sector at this stage.
